stories.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
stories.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

stories.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
stories.description: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.

stories.title: Iran attacks ships in Hormuz Strait as the U.S. continues its blockade amid ceasefire
stories.description: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.

stories.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
stories.description: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

stories.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

stories.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
stories.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

stories.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
stories.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

stories.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
stories.description: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

stories.title: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.description: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: Extension of US-Iran ceasefire gives 'breathing space'
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet reports from Tehran as Trump announces an extension of the ceasefire with Iran.

stories.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.description: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

stories.title: US intercepts and seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship, Trump says
stories.description: Tehran vows retaliation soon for what it calls an "act of armed piracy", which comes as the US prepares for a second round of talks.

stories.title: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.description: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Iran: 'Going back to war is not in anyone's interest,' risk analyst says
stories.description: A risk management analyst has spoken to FRANCE 24 about what it means, amid the ceasefire, if the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. Paymon Azmoudeh is a senior consultant with the risk and compliance practice at Forward Global. He says he thinks the calculation on all ...

stories.title: Nigeria faces jet fuel shortage as Strait of Hormuz disruption bites
stories.description: Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies of middle distillates, a fuel marketers’ group said, squeezing airlines already grappling with soaring costs. Products such as jet fuel and diesel that normally ...

stories.title: Iranian economy: Layoffs, inflation add to sanctions pain amid war
stories.description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Israel has created a profound economic crisis in Iran. As the government deals with the crippling internet blackouts and soaring inflation, a sense of uncertainty has taken hold of the nation's workforce.

stories.title: In south Lebanon village, Hezbollah buries fighters killed in war with Israel
stories.description: Hezbollah held a mass funeral in south Lebanon's Kfar Sir on Tuesday for more than a dozen fighters killed during more than six weeks of war with Israel. The ceremony came after Israel and Lebanon entered into a 10-day ceasefire on Friday announced by US President Donald Trump. I...

stories.title: Iran's workers face waves of layoffs as war batters domestic economy
stories.description: Iranians are confronting mass layoffs and soaring inflation amid destroyed infrastructure and internet blackouts. We take a closer look. But first: US senators grill Donald Trump's nominee for Federal Reserve chair, and the global condom industry takes a blow from supply chain is...

stories.title: 🔴 Middle East war live: Iran seizes two vessels in Strait of Hormuz, state media says
Trump extends ceasefire, says giving Iran time to negotiate
stories.description: President Donald Trump extended the US ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for peace talks, with Tehran silent on the decision early Wednesday. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the two week truce, crediting a request from mediator Pakistan and stressing the need to gi...

stories.title: Trump extends ceasefire with Iran, says Tehran regime 'seriously fractured'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, claiming the Islamic republic was "collapsing financially" because of the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. There was no word from Tehran on the ceasefire and an Iranian gunboat fired on a cont...

stories.title: What is uranium enrichment and how quickly could Iran build a nuclear bomb?
stories.description: Al Jazeera spoke with MIT professor Ted Postol about what Iran could do with its 440kg of 60 percent enriched uranium.

stories.title: Beyond the desecrated statue: What truly demands our outrage
stories.description: Destruction of a statue of Jesus in Lebanon is disturbing, but Israel’s genocide is what really demands our outrage.

stories.title: Iran calls US ship seizure ‘piracy’: Is it?
stories.description: Since the early days of the war on Iran, the US has been targeting Iranian ships in international waters.

stories.title: Five major issues affecting the FIFA World Cup with 50 days to go
stories.description: From the US-Israel war on Iran to exorbitant ticket prices and violence in host nations, here's what's gone wrong.

stories.title: Is China a winner of the Iran war or facing economic risks?
stories.description: China's economy beats expectations despite the Iran war, but weak demand and structural risks remain.

stories.title: Iran captures two vessels in Strait of Hormuz after ship comes under fire
stories.description: The IRGC says the aggression came in response to what it described as the US's capture of an Iranian commercial vessel.

stories.title: Israel attacks mosque in Lebanon in another ceasefire violation
stories.description: Israel attacks mosque in Lebanon in another ceasefire violation

stories.title: Israel’s ‘digital occupation’ of south Lebanon
stories.description: Al Jazeera’s Linh Nguyen explains Israeli drones above the skies in Lebanon, which critics call a "digital occupation."

stories.title: Indonesia denounces Israel ‘propaganda’ banner over Gaza hospital ruins
stories.description: Indonesia on Wednesday accused Israel of flying a “propaganda” banner over the ruins of a hospital that had been built in Gaza with Indonesian funding. The Indonesia Hospital, in the north of the Gaza Strip near the fortified border with Israel, was opened in late 2015 after Indo...

stories.title: Ink and rubble: The systematic assault on Iran’s intellectual soul
stories.description: Last year, my plans to celebrate Eid in Iran were dismantled by the sudden escalation of regional aggression. What was meant to be a personal pilgrimage became a casualty of geopolitics. Yet, in a twist of fate this past September, I found myself touching down at Tehran’s IKA Air...

stories.title: A year on, India has failed to substantiate allegations against Pakistan over Pahalgam attack: info minister
stories.description: Information Minister Attaullah Tarar on Wednesday said that even a year on, India has failed to provide evidence for its allegations against Pakistan over the Pahalgam attack. The April 22, 2025 attack in Pahalgam saw 26 people, mostly tourists, killed in what is being described ...

stories.title: Macron vows to help Lebanon prepare for talks with Israel
stories.description: • Lebanese PM says country needs €500m to address humanitarian fallout• Speaker says Israel will face resistance if troops stay in Lebanon• Belgian FM terms Tel Aviv’s conduct ‘totally unacceptable’• Israel says applying diplomatic, military pressure to disarm Hezbollah PARIS/BEI...

stories.title: ‘At the request of CDF Munir, PM Shehbaz’, Trump announces extension in ceasefire until Iran submits proposal
stories.description: Key developments: Trump has announced an extension in the ceasefire with Iran until Tehran submits a “unified proposal”; however, a naval blockade of Iranian ports will remain in place; JD Vance’s visit to Pakistan ’“not happening today”, says White House official PM Shehbaz than...

stories.title: World urges restraint as US, Iran stick to guns
stories.description: • Trump eyes ‘great deal’, claims US in ‘very strong’ negotiating position• Iran’s top negotiator slams attempt to ‘turn negotiating table into table of submission’• Iranian tanker seized; Tehran says move akin to ‘state terrorism, piracy’• US Senate to vote again on resolution s...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran Guards say 'seized' two ships attempting to cross Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran has not yet responded to U.S. President Trump’s announcement of the ceasefire extension

stories.title: India-bound ship among two vessels ‘seized’ by Iran Guards in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Liberia-flagged Epaminodas was headed to Gujarat’s Mundra port, according to ship-tracking data; both ships directed to Iranian coast

stories.title: Iran fires on three ships in Strait of Hormuz, complicating efforts to resume U.S.-Iran talks
stories.description: Iran opened fire on a container ship in the strait, and a second was attacked a short time later, according to the British military’s United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre

stories.title: Iran says U.S. naval blockade has little impact on food supply
stories.description: "Despite the U.S. naval blockade, we have no problem in supplying basic goods and food,” Iran’s Agriculture Minister, Gholamreza Nouri, said

stories.title: Iran hangs man convicted of links to Israel's Mossad
stories.description: "Mehdi Farid... was hanged this morning for extensive cooperation with the terrorist spy service Mossad after the case was examined and the final verdict was approved," the judiciary's Mizan Online website said.

stories.title: Hezbollah says attacked north Israel in response to Israeli truce violations
stories.description: It said the move came “in defence of Lebanon and its people and in response to the blatant” Israeli ceasefire violations, including “attacks on civilians and the destruction of their homes and villages”

stories.title: Seychelles grateful to Russia for support amid crisis around Iran — president
stories.description: African countries faced a sharp rise in fuel and fertilizer prices amid the crisis

stories.title: US fails to accomplish all four stated goals of Iran operation — US professor
stories.description: John Mearsheimer described the US attack on Iran as "a colossal blunder," which put President Donald Trump in a predicament

stories.title: EU ambassadors approve funding for Kiev, anti-Russian sanctions
stories.description: The Cyprus presidency in the EU said the ambassadors approved the 90 bln euro loan for Ukraine

stories.title: One of vessels detained by Iranian Navy in Hormuz bound for India — tracking service
stories.description: According to Marine Traffic, the EPAMINONDAS left the UAE's Jebel Ali port bound for India's Mundra port
